sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Aston Villa have shown solid form in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins, including a strong 4-1 victory over Newcastle United, though they suffered a 3-0 defeat to Crystal Palace. Tottenham Hotspur, on the other hand, have struggled, managing only 2 wins in their last 5, with a heavy 5-1 loss to Liverpool highlighting defensive issues. Villa's consistency gives them an edge at home.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, results are split with Aston Villa winning 2-1 at home recently and Tottenham securing a 4-1 victory in their last meeting at their ground. Matches between these teams often see goals, with both teams scoring in 4 of the last 5 encounters and over 2.5 goals in all 5.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Aston Villa (6th, 63 points) have outperformed Tottenham (17th, 38 points) significantly this season, with better attacking output (56 goals) and defensive stability. Villa rank highly in big chances created (3rd in league) and ground duels won (1st), while Tottenham's defense has leaked 59 goals, one of the worst in the league.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Aston Villa's Ollie Watkins (16 goals) and Morgan Rogers (8 goals, 9 assists) are in fine form, though missing players like Youri Tielemans could impact midfield creativity. Tottenham rely on Heung-min Son (7 goals, 9 assists) and Brennan Johnson (11 goals), but absences of James Maddison and Dejan Kulusevski weaken their attack.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction Factors
sections.text: Given Villa's superior league position, home advantage, and recent form, they are favorites. Tottenham's defensive frailties and poor away record (evident in recent heavy losses) suggest they will struggle to contain Villa's attack. Expect a high-scoring game based on historical trends and current stats.
